summary: 6 new tests added for task 4 CPU optimizations. All new tests have meaningful behavioral assertions and pass the litmus test. No critical or major issues. Minor redundancy in 2 tests (duplicate scenarios) and one weak existing assertion left unaddressed. A small edge-case gap (no-tags → returns 0) is the only missing coverage.

findings.issue: test_uppercase_doctype uses HTML '<!DOCTYPE html><body>content</body>' and asserts pos == len('<!DOCTYPE html>') — nearly identical to existing test_no_head_no_html_falls_back_to_doctype (same tag, same assertion, only body text differs). Adds no new coverage.
findings.recommendation: Replace with a test that adds actual new value: uppercase DOCTYPE without any html/head tags, e.g. '<!DOCTYPE HTML PUBLIC "-//W3C//DTD HTML 4.01//EN">' (legacy doctype) — or remove and rely on existing test. If kept, at minimum use a distinct HTML variant like one with XHTML doctype.

findings.issue: test_head_with_lang_attr uses the exact same HTML as existing test_find_head_with_attributes ('<html><head lang="en">...'). The new test does add an exact position assertion that the existing test lacks, but it is testing the same input, not a new scenario.
findings.recommendation: Either: (a) strengthen the existing test_find_head_with_attributes by adding 'assert pos == len('<html><head lang="en">')' instead of duplicating it, or (b) replace new test with a distinct variant such as '<head data-theme="dark" class="foo">' to cover multi-attribute tags.

findings.issue: Existing test_uppercase_head asserts only 'pos > 0', which passes for any non-zero return value (e.g. 1). If the function found the wrong tag and returned the wrong position, this test would still pass. Not introduced by task 4, but the new CPU tests do not fix it.
findings.recommendation: Change assertion to: assert pos == len('<HTML><HEAD>') — the exact expected position after the uppercase HEAD tag closing >.

findings.issue: No test for HTML with none of <head>, <html>, <!doctype> present. The task spec explicitly lists this edge case: 'HTML with no <head>, no <html>, no <!doctype> — must return 0'. This path exercises the final fallthrough in _find_inject_position.
findings.recommendation:
Add: def test_no_tags_returns_zero(): html = '

Plain paragraph

'; assert _find_inject_position(html) == 0

findings.issue: test_tuple_endswith_multiple tests 5 subdomains in a loop, but www.google.com is already covered by existing parametrized test_target_domains_match. The other 4 (maps, mail, drive, docs) are genuinely new, so the test has partial value.
findings.recommendation: Remove www.google.com from the list (already parametrized) and keep only the 4 domains not covered elsewhere. Alternatively, rename to test_new_subdomains_match to signal intent. Using a loop rather than parametrize also hides which subdomain fails — consider @pytest.mark.parametrize for better failure output.
